Analyst Buys.description: The Analyst Buys Strategy uses Quiver’s proprietary Analyst Scoring method, which rates Wall Street Analysts based on the accuracy of their historical price targets. We use these scores to weight each analyst’s most recent forecasts, giving a stronger weight to analysts with historical success, and lower (or in some cases, negative) weights to analysts with poor historical accuracy. We then take all forecasts made over the last year, and find the average weighted forecast for every ticker. We take the stocks with the top 100 scores, and then narrow it down to the top 10 largest by market cap (to avoid stocks with sparse analyst coverage). The strategy takes an equal-weighted position in those 10 companies, and rebalances on a monthly basis.

Bill Ackman.description: The Bill Ackman Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management using 13F filings and is rebalanced when new filings are reported.

Bill Gates.description: The Bill Gates Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of Bill Gates's Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Trust using 13F filings and is rebalanced when new filings are reported.

Cleo Fields.description: The Cleo Fields Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Cleo Fields (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Congress Buys.description: The Congress Buys Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been purchased by members of U.S. Congress (or their family). This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the purchases, with weekly rebalancing.

Congress Long-Short.description: The Congress Long-Short Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been purchased or sold by members of U.S. Congress (or their family). It takes a long position in stocks that have been purchased, and a short position in stocks which have been sold. This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the transactions and employs leverage with 130% long exposure and 30% short exposure, with weekly rebalancing.

Congress Sells.description: The Congress Sells Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been sold by members of U.S. Congress (or their family). It takes a long position in stocks that have been sold. This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the transactions, with weekly rebalancing.

Dan Meuser.description: The Dan Meuser Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Dan Meuser (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Donald Beyer.description: The Donald Beyer Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Don Beyer (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Energy and Commerce Committee (House).description: The Energy and Commerce Committee (House) Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been recently purchased by current/past members of the U.S. House's Energy and Commerce Committee (or their family). This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the purchases, with weekly rebalancing.

Homeland Security Committee (Senate).description: The Homeland Security Committee (Senate) Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been recently purchased by current/past members of the U.S. Senate's Homeland Security Committee (or their family). This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the purchases, with weekly rebalancing.

Howard Marks.description: The Howard Marks Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of Howard Marks's Oaktree Capital Management using 13F filings and is rebalanced when new filings are reported.

Insider Purchases.description: The Insider Purchases Strategy uses a proprietary model to score purchases made by Insiders within companies based on factors related to the trade, the insider, and the company. Scores are then rolled up to a company level based on a decaying trailing window, where the top 10 companies are equally weighted at the start of every week. The writeup on the methodology of this strategy can be found here .

Inverse CNBC.description: The Inverse CNBC Strategy takes a short position in CNBC's 10 most-recommended tickers (whether bullish or bearish) over the previous 90 days, and hedges it with a long position in the market index. It rebalances on a weekly basis, with equal weighting.

Inverse Cramer.description: The Inverse Cramer Strategy takes a short position in Cramer's 10 most-recommended tickers (whether bullish or bearish) over the previous 30 days, and hedges it with a long position in the market index. It rebalances on a weekly basis, with equal weighting.

Josh Gottheimer.description: The Josh Gottheimer Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Josh Gottheimer (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Lobbying Spending Growth.description: The Lobbying Spending Growth Strategy takes an equal-weighted position in 10 publicly-traded companies that disclosed the most Quarter over Quarter growth in U.S. federal lobbying expenditures, rebalanced monthly.

Michael Burry.description: The Michael Burry Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management using 13F filings and is rebalanced when new filings are reported.

Nancy Pelosi.description: The Nancy Pelosi Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Nancy Pelosi (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Rob Bresnahan.description: The Rob Bresnahan Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Rob Bresnahan (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Sector Weighted DC Insider.description: The Sector Weighted DC Insider Strategy tracks the performance of companies with a significant amount of both corporate lobbying, government contracts, and congressional stock trading. The portfolio is rebalanced at the start of every month, and weighted to match S&P 500 sector allocation.

Sheldon Whitehouse.description: The Sheldon Whitehouse Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Senator Sheldon Whitehouse (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Shelley Moore Capito.description: The Shelley Moore Capito Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Senator Shelley Moore Capito (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Tim Moore.description: The Tim Moore Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Rep. Tim Moore (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Tina Smith.description: The Tina Smith Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Senator Tina Smith (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Tom Carper.description: The Tom Carper Strategy attempts to mirror the portfolio of common stock for Senator Tom Carper (and their family) and is rebalanced when new trades or annual reports are reported.

Top Gov Contract Recipients.description: The Top Gov Contract Recipients Strategy selects the top 20 recipients of government contracts, weighted by the values of the announced contracts. Each company's government contracts are measured with an exponential moving average to favor more recent contracts, while still weighing historical contracts. This portfolio is rebalanced at the start of every month.

Top Lobbying Spenders.description: The Top Lobbying Spenders Strategy takes an equal-weighted position in 10 publicly-traded companies that have reported the most lobbying spending over the last quarter, rebalanced monthly.

Transportation and Infra. Committee (Hou.description: The Transportation and Infra. Committee (House) Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been recently purchased by current/past members of the U.S. House's Transportation and Infrastructure Committee (or their family). This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the purchases, with weekly rebalancing.

U.S. House Long-Short.description: The U.S. House Long-Short Strategy tracks the performance of stocks that have been recently purchased or sold by members of the U.S. House (or their family). It takes a long position in stocks that have been purchased, and a short position in stocks which have been sold. This strategy is weighted based on the reported size of the transactions and employs leverage with 130% long exposure and 30% short exposure, with weekly rebalancing.

Wall Street Conviction.description: The Wall Street Conviction Strategy uses 13F filings for institutions holdings over $100M in securities to find the stock that each fund has the highest conviction on within the S&P500 universe. Conviction here is measured as the percent allocation in their portfolio less the allocation in the S&P500. The highest-conviction stock for all funds are aggregated to form the equal-weighted selected stocks for this strategy. Positions are rebalanced quarterly, 47 days after the end of the previous quarter to allow time for funds to file. The basis of this strategy is detailed in the whitepaper: Systematic 13F Hedge Fund Alpha .
